operating_state: Total revenue increased 14% to $6,655 million driven by semiconductor solutions growth reflecting higher wireless content in mobile handsets with elevated quarterly shipments from later-than-typical major customer ramp, plus increased networking and broadband demand partially offset by lower server storage demand. Infrastructure software growth came from mainframe solutions, FC SAN products and enterprise security solutions. The company operates primarily outsourced manufacturing with TSMC producing approximately 88% of semiconductor wafers.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded to 59% from 56% primarily due to lower amortization of acquisition-related intangible assets and infrastructure software cost structure improvements. Operating expenses decreased significantly with selling, general and administrative expense falling due to lower acquisition-related costs from prior year Symantec Business acquisition and reduced compensation expense from completed integration. Stock-based compensation expense decreased reflecting full vesting of certain equity awards and forfeitures.
material_events: Broadcom completed major debt refinancing including new senior notes with maturities from 2028 to 2051 and replacement of revolving credit facility with new facility. The company completed early settlement of private exchange offers exchanging existing notes for new notes due 2033 and 2034. California Institute of Technology was awarded $270.2 million in past damages from patent infringement judgment under appeal. The company paid cash dividends totaling $1,543 million.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 continues creating supply chain constraints including extended lead times for wafers and substrates, with the company building products to order rather than forecasts due to ongoing uncertainty. Export restrictions on larger customers including suspended sales to Huawei have adversely impacted revenue. Escalating U.S.-China trade tensions resulted in restrictions and increased tariffs harming ability to participate in Chinese markets with Malaysia-based operations facing potential pandemic-related disruption risks.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures for fiscal 2021 slightly higher than fiscal 2020 while maintaining strategy of building semiconductor products to order given macroeconomic uncertainty. Customer concentration continues with Apple representing approximately 25% of revenue and top five customers exceeding 35%. Growth strategy includes acquiring businesses offering complementary products and technologies while maintaining quarterly dividend policy and significant research and development investments.
uncertainty_profile: Pending appeal of $270.2 million patent infringement judgment creates significant financial exposure alongside ongoing FTC investigation into contracting and business practices. Export license applications for Huawei sales remain uncertain with potential for expanded U.S. restrictions on Chinese technology companies. Supply chain disruptions may limit ability to fulfill orders while tax incentives expire in Singapore in 2025 and Malaysia in 2028 with potential material impact on effective tax rates.
